# Normalize routing weights with Sinkhorn iterations

<!-- Generated by scripts/generate_deepseek_manual.py; do not edit. -->

This multi-head composition translation expresses mixing, normalization, and recompute stages as tile dataflow.

| --- | --- |
| Upstream module | [`tile_kernels/mhc/sinkhorn_kernel.py`](https://github.com/deepseek-ai/TileKernels/blob/36d9e45d38e204ebb87e6f6e833821eee0482fe5/tile_kernels/mhc/sinkhorn_kernel.py) |

## Core C++ kernel

```cpp title="sinkhorn_pto.hpp" linenums="1"
template <int NumTokens, int Hidden, int Repeat = 1, int TileM = 16>
void sinkhorn_fwd(float *comb_res_mix, float *comb_res_mix_out) {
    static_assert(NumTokens > 0 && Hidden > 0 && Repeat >= 1, "dim must be positive");
    static_assert(TileM % 8 == 0, "TileM must be multiple of 8");
    constexpr float kEps = 1e-20f;
    constexpr int kTiles = Hidden / TileM;
    using namespace pto;
    using gm_t = global_tensor<float, RowMajor<NumTokens * Hidden, Hidden>>;
    using tile_mat = LocalTile< float, TileM, TileM, BLayout::RowMajor>;
    using tile_vec = LocalTile< float, TileM, 8, BLayout::RowMajor, TileM, 1>;
    using tile_cvec = LocalTile< float, 1, TileM, BLayout::RowMajor, 1, TileM>;
    using it_t = global_iterator<gm_t, tile_mat>;
    it_t in_iter(comb_res_mix); it_t out_iter(comb_res_mix_out);

    for (int n = 0; n < NumTokens; ++n) {
        for (int ti = 0; ti < kTiles; ++ti) {
            for (int tj = 0; tj < kTiles; ++tj) {
                auto gin = in_iter(n * kTiles + ti, tj); auto gout = out_iter(n * kTiles + ti, tj);
                tile_mat x, t; tile_vec rmax, rsum, denom, recip; tile_cvec csum, cdenom, crecip;
                TLOAD(x, gin);

                TROWMAX(rmax, x);
                TROWEXPANDSUB(t, x, rmax);
                TEXP(x, t);
                TROWSUM(rsum, x);
                TADDS(denom, rsum, kEps);
                TRECIP(recip, denom);
                TROWEXPANDMUL(t, x, recip);
                TADDS(x, t, kEps);

                TCOLSUM(csum, x);
                TADDS(cdenom, csum, kEps);
                TRECIP(crecip, cdenom);
                TCOLEXPANDMUL(x, x, crecip);

                for (int r = 1; r < Repeat; ++r) {
                    TROWSUM(rsum, x);
                    TADDS(denom, rsum, kEps);
                    TRECIP(recip, denom);
                    TROWEXPANDMUL(t, x, recip);
                    TCOLSUM(csum, t);
                    TADDS(cdenom, csum, kEps);
                    TRECIP(crecip, cdenom);
                    TCOLEXPANDMUL(x, t, crecip);
                }
                TSTORE(gout, x);
            }
        }
    }
}
```

## Algorithm walkthrough

Load hidden-state and coefficient panels, compose matrix, reduction, and pointwise operations, and write the resulting head or gradient panel.

## Source-to-intrinsic mapping

[`TADDS`](../../../intrinsics/tadds.md), [`TCOLEXPANDMUL`](../../../intrinsics/tcolexpandmul.md), [`TCOLSUM`](../../../intrinsics/tcolsum.md), [`TEXP`](../../../intrinsics/texp.md), [`TLOAD`](../../../intrinsics/tload.md), [`TRECIP`](../../../intrinsics/trecip.md), [`TROWEXPANDMUL`](../../../intrinsics/trowexpandmul.md), [`TROWEXPANDSUB`](../../../intrinsics/trowexpandsub.md), [`TROWMAX`](../../../intrinsics/trowmax.md), [`TROWSUM`](../../../intrinsics/trowsum.md), [`TSTORE`](../../../intrinsics/tstore.md)

Scalar C++ controls dimensions, loop bounds, and tile coordinates. Tensor
values remain in global tensor views and typed tiles throughout the data path.

## Block and thread partition

Blocks partition the outer token range; threads partition head or hidden dimensions into disjoint fragments of at least 128 bytes.

## Memory and tile flow

Global panels enter local tiles, matrix and reduction stages produce typed intermediates, and block-shared tiles are used when a value must be visible across the group.

## Build and verification

Run the migration gate from the repository root:

```console
python3 scripts/verify_deepseek_migration.py
```

The verification label records the strongest completed gate. A
`source-validated` page is a valid API example even when compiler lowering
for one of its public intrinsics is not yet available.

    #ifndef SUPERNPU_SINKHORN_PTO_HPP
    #define SUPERNPU_SINKHORN_PTO_HPP
    #include <common/pto_tileop.hpp>
    #include <cstddef>
    #include <cstdint>

    namespace supernpu::tile_isa {

    template <int NumTokens, int Hidden, int Repeat = 1, int TileM = 16>
    void sinkhorn_fwd(float *comb_res_mix, float *comb_res_mix_out) {
        static_assert(NumTokens > 0 && Hidden > 0 && Repeat >= 1, "dim must be positive");
        static_assert(TileM % 8 == 0, "TileM must be multiple of 8");
        constexpr float kEps = 1e-20f;
        constexpr int kTiles = Hidden / TileM;
        using namespace pto;
        using gm_t = global_tensor<float, RowMajor<NumTokens * Hidden, Hidden>>;
        using tile_mat = LocalTile< float, TileM, TileM, BLayout::RowMajor>;
        using tile_vec = LocalTile< float, TileM, 8, BLayout::RowMajor, TileM, 1>;
        using tile_cvec = LocalTile< float, 1, TileM, BLayout::RowMajor, 1, TileM>;
        using it_t = global_iterator<gm_t, tile_mat>;
        it_t in_iter(comb_res_mix); it_t out_iter(comb_res_mix_out);

        for (int n = 0; n < NumTokens; ++n) {
            for (int ti = 0; ti < kTiles; ++ti) {
                for (int tj = 0; tj < kTiles; ++tj) {
                    auto gin = in_iter(n * kTiles + ti, tj); auto gout = out_iter(n * kTiles + ti, tj);
                    tile_mat x, t; tile_vec rmax, rsum, denom, recip; tile_cvec csum, cdenom, crecip;
                    TLOAD(x, gin);

                    TROWMAX(rmax, x);
                    TROWEXPANDSUB(t, x, rmax);
                    TEXP(x, t);
                    TROWSUM(rsum, x);
                    TADDS(denom, rsum, kEps);
                    TRECIP(recip, denom);
                    TROWEXPANDMUL(t, x, recip);
                    TADDS(x, t, kEps);

                    TCOLSUM(csum, x);
                    TADDS(cdenom, csum, kEps);
                    TRECIP(crecip, cdenom);
                    TCOLEXPANDMUL(x, x, crecip);

                    for (int r = 1; r < Repeat; ++r) {
                        TROWSUM(rsum, x);
                        TADDS(denom, rsum, kEps);
                        TRECIP(recip, denom);
                        TROWEXPANDMUL(t, x, recip);
                        TCOLSUM(csum, t);
                        TADDS(cdenom, csum, kEps);
                        TRECIP(crecip, cdenom);
                        TCOLEXPANDMUL(x, t, crecip);
                    }
                    TSTORE(gout, x);
                }
            }
        }
    }

    }
    #endif
